question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user call findNetworkNodes() with no filters (empty nodeIds[], minNodeId=0, maxNodeId=Long.MAX_VALUE) to enumerate all active network nodes and their serviceEndpointsJson (IP addresses and ports), then use that information to launch targeted denial-of-service attacks against >= 30% of nodes, causing those nodes to go offline without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the serviceEndpointsJson field returned by findNetworkNodes() — which contains ip_address_v4 and port for every node — to compile a complete map of network node endpoints and selectively flood >= 30% of those endpoints with application-layer traffic, shutting them down without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the grpcProxyEndpointJson field returned by findNetworkNodes() to discover the gRPC proxy domain names and ports of network nodes, then craft malformed gRPC requests to those endpoints to crash >= 30% of nodes without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the adminKey bytes returned in the adminKey field of NetworkNodeDto to reconstruct or impersonate node administrative credentials, then issue shutdown or deregistration commands to >= 30% of network nodes without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve the publicKey field for all nodes via findNetworkNodes() and use those public keys to identify cryptographic weaknesses or perform targeted key-confusion attacks against >= 30% of nodes, causing them to reject legitimate consensus messages and effectively shut down?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve the nodeCertHash field for all nodes via findNetworkNodes() and use those certificate hashes to perform certificate-pinning bypass or certificate revocation attacks against >= 30% of nodes, causing TLS handshake failures that shut down node communication?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an arbitrarily large Long[] nodeIds array to findNetworkNodes() — since there is no array-size validation visible in the repository interface — causing the PostgreSQL `= any(:nodeIds)` clause to consume excessive memory and CPU, exhausting database resources and indirectly causing >= 30% of network nodes to lose their mirror node data feed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass orderDirection as an arbitrary string value other than 'ASC' or 'DESC' to findNetworkNodes(), causing the CASE expression `case when :orderDirection = 'ASC' then abe.node_id end asc` to produce a null sort that returns nodes in an undefined order, and if combined with a crafted limit, cause the caller to act on a misleading node list that omits critical nodes from monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit concurrent high-frequency requests to findNetworkNodes() with minNodeId=0 and maxNodeId=Long.MAX_VALUE to exhaust the PostgreSQL connection pool, causing the mirror node to become unresponsive and depriving >= 30% of network nodes of their data synchronization service?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the latest_address_book CTE — which selects `order by start_consensus_timestamp desc limit 1` — by flooding the address_book table with crafted entries (if any write path exists) to cause findNetworkNodes() to return a stale or attacker-controlled address book, misleading operators about which nodes are active and causing >= 30% of nodes to be incorrectly decommissioned?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the nodeAccountId field returned by findNetworkNodes() — which resolves via `coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id)` — to identify the Hedera account IDs of network nodes and then submit malicious transactions targeting those accounts to freeze or drain them, causing >= 30% of nodes to lose their operational accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an extremely large limit value to findNetworkNodes() — if the MAX_LIMIT cap in NetworkNodeRequest.getEffectiveLimit() is not enforced at the repository layer — causing the query to return an unbounded result set that exhausts JVM heap memory and crashes the mirror node, depriving network nodes of their data service?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass minNodeId=Long.MAX_VALUE and maxNodeId=0 (inverted range) to findNetworkNodes(), causing the WHERE clause `abe.node_id >= :minNodeId AND abe.node_id <= :maxNodeId` to always evaluate false and return an empty result, and if this empty result is consumed by an automated health-check system, cause it to incorrectly report all nodes as offline and trigger a mass shutdown of >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the stake, maxStake, minStake, and stakeRewarded fields returned by findNetworkNodes() to identify the highest-stake nodes in the network, then selectively target those high-stake nodes for disruption to cause >= 30% of consensus weight to go offline without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the latest_node_stake CTE — which uses `where consensus_timestamp = (select max(consensus_timestamp) from node_stake)` — by timing requests during a node_stake table update to receive inconsistent stake data, and use that inconsistency to mislead staking decisions that cause >= 30% of nodes to lose delegated stake and become ineligible for consensus?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a very large Long[] nodeIds array (e.g., millions of entries) to findNetworkNodes() to trigger a PostgreSQL query plan regression where the `= any(:nodeIds)` operator causes a sequential scan of address_book_entry, consuming all available I/O and CPU and causing the database to become unresponsive, indirectly shutting down >= 30% of nodes that depend on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the associatedRegisteredNodes field returned by findNetworkNodes() to map the relationships between network nodes and registered nodes, then selectively attack the registered nodes associated with >= 30% of network nodes to cause those network nodes to lose their registration and be excluded from consensus?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all nodes via findNetworkNodes() with no filters, then use the returned ip_address_v4 values from serviceEndpointsJson to perform BGP hijacking or DNS poisoning against the IP ranges of >= 30% of nodes, causing those nodes to lose network connectivity without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the port values from serviceEndpointsJson returned by findNetworkNodes() to identify non-standard or vulnerable service ports on network nodes, then exploit known vulnerabilities on those ports to shut down >= 30% of nodes without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass fileId values other than 102 to findNetworkNodes() to enumerate address books from different file IDs, discovering historical or alternative node configurations that reveal decommissioned nodes still running, and use that information to attack >= 30% of the active network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the correlated subquery `select jsonb_agg(...) from address_book_service_endpoint abse where abse.consensus_timestamp = abe.consensus_timestamp and abse.node_id = abe.node_id` by sending many concurrent requests, causing N+1 query behavior that exhausts database connections and crashes the mirror node, depriving >= 30% of network nodes of their synchronization service?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the declineReward field returned by findNetworkNodes() to identify nodes that have opted out of rewards — potentially indicating nodes with weaker economic incentives to stay online — and selectively target those nodes for disruption to achieve >= 30% node shutdown with minimal effort?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass negative values for minNodeId or maxNodeId to findNetworkNodes() — since the parameters are typed as long (primitive) with no explicit non-negative validation at the repository layer — causing unexpected query behavior that returns incorrect node sets and misleads automated systems into shutting down >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the rewardRateStart field returned by findNetworkNodes() to identify nodes with anomalous reward rates, infer which nodes are under-performing or misconfigured, and use that intelligence to target those nodes for disruption, causing >= 30% of nodes to go offline?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `convert_from(abe.node_cert_hash, 'UTF8')` expression in the nodeCertHash CASE block by triggering it on binary data that is not valid UTF-8, causing a PostgreSQL encoding error that crashes the query and makes the mirror node unable to serve node data, indirectly causing >= 30% of nodes to lose monitoring coverage?
